title: PATH being reset
body:
**Description**

`$PATH` is being reset from the image and container config.

This is possibly related to https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4812

**Steps to reproduce the issubuildae:**
1. `buildah from rust:buster`
2. `buildah config $c` (Notice `Config.config.Env` and `Config.container_config.Env`)
3. `buildah run $c -- env`

The config lists:
 `PATH=/usr/local/cargo/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin` 
but `env` shows: 
`PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin`. 

No other environment changes were found. 
(The host was 
`PATH=/home/astraluma/.cargo/bin:/home/astraluma/.poetry/bin:/home/astraluma/.local/bin:/home/astraluma/.gem/ruby/2.3.0/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/local/games:/usr/games:/home/astraluma/.local/xonsh/bin:/home/astraluma/.cargo/bin:/usr/sbin`)

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/unknown,now 1.13.2~1 amd64 [installed]
buildah/unknown 1.13.2~1 arm64
buildah/unknown 1.12.0-6 armhf
buildah/testing 1.10.1-5 i386
buildah/unknown 1.13.2~1 ppc64el
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.13.2
Go Version:      go1.13.6
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.1.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Sat Feb  1 11:04:36 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux bullseye/sid"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
ID=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux cayde7 5.4.0-3-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 5.4.13-1 (2020-01-19) x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
Seems to work with 1.12.0 on fedora 31

$ buildah --version
buildah version 1.12.0 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.1-dev)

$ buildah run $c env
PATH=/usr/local/cargo/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

Broken in master.
 ./buildah --version
buildah version 1.15.0-dev (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.1-dev)
[dwalsh@localhost buildah]$ ./buildah run $c env
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

It's probably end of day brain, but just want to check what I'm seeing.
When I do:

```
buildah from rust:buster
buildah run rust-working-container -- env

buildah config rust-working-container
buildah run rust-working-container -- env
```

The path I'm seeing does not have `/usr/local/cargo/bin` either before or after the config is run.  However, if I do : `buildah inspect rust-working-container | grep PATH`  I see `/usr/local/cargo/bin` in that output.  

Is that what you two are seeing?  Based on the description, I thought `cargo/bin` would be shown before the `buildah config` call, but not after.
I should have said if I do the inspect command before or after the config, I'm seeing `cargo/bin`

I think you got some end of the day brain. I did not mean "the right invocation of commands will change PATH as a side-effect". I did mean "depending on how you ask, PATH will have different values".

And some of these values are wrong. (That is, they don't agree with the PATH defined in the image.)
Bisecting points to #2011 as the point where this breaks.  @TomSweeneyRedHat mind if I take this one?
I just want to thank you for fixing this because I keep bumping into it and I cannot wait for a release.

That PR also fixes this case, right?

```
🐚 c=`buildah from rust:buster`
🐚 buildah config --env PATH=spam:eggs $c
🐚 buildah run $c /usr/bin/env
RUSTUP_HOME=/usr/local/rustup
CARGO_HOME=/usr/local/cargo
RUST_VERSION=1.41.0
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
HOSTNAME=
HOME=/root
```
